headline: Coinbase lays off 700 employees, cites AI-driven workflow transformation
excerpt: Armstrong said the layoffs are partly due to a crypto downturn. Yet the main motivator is making the company's leadership structure flatter, enabling its employees to work fast, with AI at the forefront.
notes: Restructuring complete Q2 2026, creating 'AI-native pods'

headline: Freshworks lays off 500 workers (11% of workforce) citing AI code automation
excerpt: CEO Dennis Woodside said that more than half of their code is written by AI
notes: AI automation eliminated 'rote work', $8M restructuring cost

headline: Coinbase cuts 700 jobs (14% of workforce) in AI-driven restructuring
excerpt: Armstrong described the decision as necessary to position the firm for its 'next phase of growth'
notes: Flattening org chart to 5 management layers, creating 'AI-native pods', $50-60M restructuring charges

headline: Meta to lay off 8,000 employees, freeze 6,000 open roles amid AI investment surge
excerpt: Meta plans to lay off about 10% of its workforce on May 20, with CEO Mark Zuckerberg attributing the layoffs to increased capital spending for AI.
notes: Layoffs linked to AI infrastructure investment

headline: Meta to lay off 8,000 employees, citing AI infrastructure costs
excerpt: Meta chief Mark Zuckerberg acknowledged his company’s spending on artificial intelligence has contributed to layoffs at Meta, according to a recording of an internal Thursday meeting reported by The Wall Street Journal.
notes: Layoffs attributed to increased AI infrastructure investments.

headline: Oracle to cut over 20,000 jobs amid AI pivot
excerpt: Oracle announced global layoffs affecting 20,000 to 30,000 employees across multiple regions, as the company invests heavily in artificial intelligence and automates workflows.
notes: Layoffs part of AI-focused restructuring.

headline: Meta announces 8,000 job cuts effective May 20 as part of AI efficiency push
excerpt: Meta announced on April 26 that it will cut approximately 8,000 jobs—roughly 10% of its workforce—starting May 20, 2026.
notes: Company spending $135B on AI while cutting workforce; official cuts begin May 20

headline: Nike announces 1,400 job cuts concentrated in technology department
excerpt: Nike announced a new round of layoffs Thursday affecting approximately 1,400 employees across the company, mostly concentrated in its technology department.
notes: Tech-focused cuts announced April 25; COO cited impact on affected teams

headline: Microsoft offers voluntary buyouts to 7% of staff amid AI restructuring
excerpt: Microsoft said Thursday it would offer buyouts to 7% of staff.
notes: Voluntary buyouts targeting 7% of ~220K workforce; announced April 24

headline: Meta to lay off 8,000 employees as part of AI efficiency push
excerpt: Meta announced plans to lay off approximately 8,000 employees, representing about 10% of its workforce, in a move aimed at boosting efficiency amid rising artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure costs.
notes: Layoffs part of strategic shift towards AI investments.

headline: Microsoft offers buyouts to 7% of U.S. staff amid AI restructuring
excerpt: Microsoft announced it is offering buyouts to about 7% of its U.S. staff, or approximately 8,750 jobs, as part of broader restructuring efforts focused on artificial intelligence.
notes: Buyouts offered as part of AI-driven restructuring.

headline: Vianai Systems reduces workforce citing AI efficiency
excerpt: Vianai Systems, an enterprise AI company, cut ~90% of its engineering team amid mounting uncertainty over funding and product direction. Irony: the company's core product was an AI productivity platform for enterprises.
notes: Vianai Systems, an enterprise AI company, cut ~90% of its engineering team amid mounting uncertainty over funding and product direction. Irony: the company's core product was an AI productivity platform for enterprises.

headline: Cars.com cuts 187 jobs amid AI transition
excerpt: Cars.com cut 187 employees (~11% of workforce) as its AI-powered advertising and marketplace platform reduced headcount needs in sales and operations.
notes: Cars.com cut 187 employees (~11% of workforce) as its AI-powered advertising and marketplace platform reduced headcount needs in sales and operations.

headline: Vimeo cuts 123 jobs amid AI transition
excerpt: Video platform Vimeo cut ~25% of its New York staff after being acquired by Italian firm Mediaforeurope. Cuts reflect platform consolidation as AI video tools reduce need for human-led production services.
notes: Video platform Vimeo cut ~25% of its New York staff after being acquired by Italian firm Mediaforeurope. Cuts reflect platform consolidation as AI video tools reduce need for human-led production services.

headline: Kintsugi reduces workforce citing AI efficiency
excerpt: Mental health voice-biomarker startup Kintsugi shut down entirely, laying off all staff. Company had raised significant VC funding to use AI to detect depression and anxiety via voice analysis, but could not achieve product-market fit.
notes: Mental health voice-biomarker startup Kintsugi shut down entirely, laying off all staff. Company had raised significant VC funding to use AI to detect depression and anxiety via voice analysis, but could not achieve product-market fit.

headline: Sonos reduces workforce citing AI efficiency
excerpt: Sonos made cuts in its marketing division as new CMO reshaped the department, partly citing AI tools enabling smaller marketing teams to do more. Company still recovering from its disastrous 2024 app redesign.
notes: Sonos made cuts in its marketing division as new CMO reshaped the department, partly citing AI tools enabling smaller marketing teams to do more. Company still recovering from its disastrous 2024 app redesign.

headline: Monzo cuts 50 jobs amid AI transition
excerpt: UK digital bank Monzo shut down its US operations entirely, laying off ~50 employees. The company is refocusing on the UK and European markets where its AI-driven banking features have stronger traction.
notes: UK digital bank Monzo shut down its US operations entirely, laying off ~50 employees. The company is refocusing on the UK and European markets where its AI-driven banking features have stronger traction.

headline: Ericsson cuts 1,600 jobs amid AI transition
excerpt: Ericsson announced 1,600 job cuts in Sweden, doubling down on cost-saving measures after years of 5G spending slowdown and US tariff headwinds. AI-driven network tools are reducing the need for traditional RF engineering and support roles.
notes: Ericsson announced 1,600 job cuts in Sweden, doubling down on cost-saving measures after years of 5G spending slowdown and US tariff headwinds. AI-driven network tools are reducing the need for traditional RF engineering and support roles.

headline: Kaseya cuts 250 jobs amid AI transition
excerpt: IT management vendor Kaseya (owner of Datto, ConnectWise rival) cut 250 employees in its 4th round since April 2024, primarily in sales, marketing, and admin. Cuts reflect Datto acquisition integration and shift to AI-automated MSP tooling.
notes: IT management vendor Kaseya (owner of Datto, ConnectWise rival) cut 250 employees in its 4th round since April 2024, primarily in sales, marketing, and admin. Cuts reflect Datto acquisition integration and shift to AI-automated MSP tooling.

headline: Multiverse cuts 50 jobs amid AI transition
excerpt: London-based edtech Multiverse (corporate apprenticeship platform backed by Euan Blair) cut dozens of jobs amid widening losses. The apprenticeship market is being disrupted by AI upskilling tools that reduce need for structured human-led programs.
notes: London-based edtech Multiverse (corporate apprenticeship platform backed by Euan Blair) cut dozens of jobs amid widening losses. The apprenticeship market is being disrupted by AI upskilling tools that reduce need for structured human-led programs.

headline: HPE (Hewlett Packard Enterprise) cuts 2,500 jobs amid AI transition
excerpt: HPE CEO Antonio Neri announced 2,500 layoffs over 18 months, part of broader cost-cutting while completing the Juniper Networks acquisition. HPE is restructuring around AI-era infrastructure (networking, cloud) and cutting traditional IT support and sales roles.
notes: HPE CEO Antonio Neri announced 2,500 layoffs over 18 months, part of broader cost-cutting while completing the Juniper Networks acquisition. HPE is restructuring around AI-era infrastructure (networking, cloud) and cutting traditional IT support and sales roles.

headline: Autodesk cuts 1,350 jobs amid AI transition
excerpt: Autodesk cut ~1,350 employees (9% of workforce), citing AI-driven cloud and platform products as most profitable. CEO Andrew Anagnost noted fewer sales staff needed as subscription models and AI self-service tools grow. Company shifting investment toward AI-augmented design tools.
notes: Autodesk cut ~1,350 employees (9% of workforce), citing AI-driven cloud and platform products as most profitable. CEO Andrew Anagnost noted fewer sales staff needed as subscription models and AI self-service tools grow. Company shifting investment toward AI-augmented design tools.

headline: HP Inc. cuts 2,000 jobs amid AI transition
excerpt: HP cut up to 2,000 more workers as part of ongoing multi-year restructuring. Earlier HP had controversially tried to eliminate phone support by forcing 15-minute waits — a failed attempt to use friction to push users to AI self-service. Cuts continue as printing division shrinks.
notes: HP cut up to 2,000 more workers as part of ongoing multi-year restructuring. Earlier HP had controversially tried to eliminate phone support by forcing 15-minute waits — a failed attempt to use friction to push users to AI self-service. Cuts continue as printing division shrinks.

headline: CISA cuts 130 jobs amid AI transition
excerpt: US Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency lost 130 staff via DOGE-driven cuts. Cybersecurity experts warned the losses would harm international threat-intelligence collaborations. One of the few government AI-efficiency-adjacent layoffs with documented national security concerns.
notes: US Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency lost 130 staff via DOGE-driven cuts. Cybersecurity experts warned the losses would harm international threat-intelligence collaborations. One of the few government AI-efficiency-adjacent layoffs with documented national security concerns.

headline: Workday cuts 1,750 jobs amid AI transition
excerpt: Workday CEO Carl Eschenbach cut 1,750 employees (8.5% of workforce) to fund AI investment and global expansion, disposing of unused office space. Analysts flagged that AI — in the form of Workday's own HRIS automation — was reducing staffing needs in HR-adjacent roles internally.
notes: Workday CEO Carl Eschenbach cut 1,750 employees (8.5% of workforce) to fund AI investment and global expansion, disposing of unused office space. Analysts flagged that AI — in the form of Workday's own HRIS automation — was reducing staffing needs in HR-adjacent roles internally.

headline: Salesforce cuts 1,000 jobs amid AI transition
excerpt: Salesforce cut 1,000+ workers across the company (per Bloomberg) while simultaneously hiring sales staff for new Agentforce AI products. CEO Benioff: the company hired 'zero new engineers in FY26' because AI coding agents handle capacity. Classic dual motion: cut human roles, sell AI to others.
notes: Salesforce cut 1,000+ workers across the company (per Bloomberg) while simultaneously hiring sales staff for new Agentforce AI products. CEO Benioff: the company hired 'zero new engineers in FY26' because AI coding agents handle capacity. Classic dual motion: cut human roles, sell AI to others.

headline: Meta cuts 3,600 jobs amid AI transition
excerpt: CEO Zuckerberg told staff he would 'move out low performers faster' — cutting 5% (~3,600 employees) beginning Feb 10. Company had already reduced headcount 5% via attrition in 2024. US fact-checkers replaced by AI-assisted community notes system. Zuckerberg framed it as building a 'more meritocratic' organization.

excerpt: Small round of cuts across gaming & sales teams; part of ongoing AI-driven restructuring entering 2025.

excerpt: ~22% of staff cut; CEO cited students switching to free AI tools like ChatGPT instead of Chegg's paid platform.

headline: Equinix cuts 400 jobs amid AI transition
excerpt: Equinix cut ~400 of its 13,000 employees despite record demand for its data center capacity. New CEO Adaire Fox-Martin restructuring post-leadership shakeup (CIO and CISO both departed). Paradox: AI is driving record demand for Equinix facilities while AI tools reduce its internal headcount needs.

headline: AMD cuts 1,000 jobs amid AI transition
excerpt: AMD cut ~1,000 employees (4% of workforce) to reallocate resources toward AI chip development. Gaming segment expected to fall 59% in 2024 while AI chip sales target $5B. AMD's MI300X AI accelerator competes with Nvidia's H100/H200 but Nvidia holds 80%+ market share. Stock down 5% vs Nvidia's +200% in 2024.

headline: Freshworks cuts 660 jobs amid AI transition
excerpt: Freshworks cut 660 employees (13% of workforce) despite reporting strong earnings. Analyst: 'AI quite likely played a key role.'

excerpt: Cisco's second major 2024 round — 6,000 layoffs (7% of workforce) following 4,200 in February. Divisions cut include threat intel unit Talos Security. CEO Chuck Robbins explicitly cited need to 'restructure product groups' around AI-era networking. Total 2024 Cisco cuts: 10,200+.

headline: General Motors cuts 1,000 jobs amid AI transition
excerpt: GM cut 1,000+ software and services staff as it reassessed its digital transformation strategy. Internal memo: moving resources to 'highest-priority work and flattening hierarchies.' GM had over-hired for its Cruise autonomous vehicle unit (before Cruise's collapse) and software-defined vehicle initiatives.

headline: Intel cuts 15,000 jobs amid AI transition
excerpt: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger announced 15,000 layoffs (15% of workforce) after Q2 revenue stagnated and net income fell 85%. excerpt: UPS cut 12,000 of its 85,000 management jobs explicitly because AI tools are replacing coordination and scheduling functions. CEO Carol Tomé also imposed 5-day office return. excerpt: Google cut 12,000 employees (6% of workforce) — largest in company history at that point. CEO Sundar Pichai: 'I take full responsibility.' Hiring surge during COVID years reversed. headline: GitLab cuts 14% of workforce (~350 employees) in AI pivot to 'agentic era'
excerpt: Developer platform GitLab has laid off about 14% of its workforce, about 350 employees, as part of a broader restructuring effort... as it exited 22 countries, flattened management layers, and invested in infrastructure to scale its platform and serve increased traffic from AI workflows.
notes: Announced alongside Q1 FY27 earnings on June 2, 2026. CEO Bill Staples cited 'agentic era' transformation; ~$30-35M restructuring charge. Savings reinvested in Duo Agent Platform and AI infrastructure. Exit from 22 countries.

Coincides with launch of AI-powered Solution Design Studio and Manhattan Active platform. AI causality inferred, not explicitly stated; $7-9M Q2 severance charge.

excerpt: The primary reason behind the restructuring was the increase in AI automation, the sources said, adding that the startup's enrollments are down 20-25% in 2026 from the previous year.

headline: Uber cuts 23% of People & Places division (~340 jobs) amid AI productivity push
excerpt: Uber, a company that exhausted its entire 2026 AI coding budget in just four months, announced it was cutting 23% of the division responsible for its own human workforce.
notes: Uber explicitly stated the cuts are unrelated to AI, but the layoffs came days after the company exhausted its 2026 AI coding budget and last month said it would slow hiring due to internal AI use. CEO Khosrowshahi previously stated AI will replace 70-80% of human work in 10 years. Estimated headcount at ~340 (upper bound of 'well under 1%' of 34,000 employees). Marked inferred due to Uber's explicit denial.

excerpt: GDC is using AI across all aspects of the business, including marketing, sales, and coding; 80% of new code is being generated by AI. GDC is 'resetting our team structures, roles, and processes to fit an AI-first world,' and the company expects annual savings of about $13 million.

headline: Salesforce cuts 86 California employees in third AI-era layoff round, targeting Agentforce AI and Marketing Cloud teams
excerpt: On June 10, 2026, Salesforce Inc CRM announced a new round of layoffs affecting 86 employees in California, marking the third such reduction since September of the previous year. This strategic move reflects the software giant's ongoing efforts to optimize its organizational structure in the age of artificial intelligence. The layoffs specifically target areas such as Agentforce AI products and Marketing Cloud software.
notes: Third reduction since September 2025; cuts span sales, administration, technology, and product teams within Agentforce AI, Mulesoft IT and Marketing Cloud software products. Confirmed via Crunchbase News tracker for week ending June 10, 2026.

headline: Google trims Cloud and Threat Intelligence Group workforce as it prioritizes AI investments
excerpt: In its efforts to prioritize its investments in AI, Mountain View, California-based internet giant Google has been paring the workforce in its Google Cloud division lately, with its Threat Intelligence Group also getting trimmed. It's unclear exactly how many of the workers in these divisions were let go.
notes: Headcount estimate is conservative (~50) as exact numbers were not disclosed. Reported in Crunchbase tracker week ending June 10, 2026. AI investment prioritization explicitly cited.

headline: Expeditors International cuts 230 tech jobs, ending no-layoff tradition after crediting AI for productivity gains
excerpt: Weeks after crediting AI for productivity gains, Expeditors International filed a WARN notice to cut 230 tech jobs and end its no-layoff policy. The freight forwarder is restructuring its U.S. global technology department, with all cuts expected to be finalized by Dec. 31. Positions impacted included software developers, quality assurance engineers, project managers and business analysts. CFO David Hackett attributed productivity improvements to artificial intelligence deployments.
notes: Seattle-based freight forwarder Expeditors International filed a WARN notice on Monday June 8, 2026, breaking a four-decade no-layoff tradition. CEO Daniel Wall linked reductions to automation and new digital tools. Cuts represent 15.4% of the company's information systems branch as of March 31, 2026. Affected offices: Seattle (68), Federal Way (66), Lynnwood (59), Bellevue (35), Airway Heights (2). Separations begin Aug. 8 and complete by Dec. 31, 2026.

headline: ServiceNow lays off hundreds of employees, cites 'real AI efficiencies' inside its own business
excerpt: ServiceNow is laying off hundreds of employees. A person familiar with the matter confirmed that a three-figure number of employees at the ITSM company are being let go. The company said its platform is generating 'real AI efficiencies' inside its own business and that it is 'actively investing in and hiring for the AI-focused skills this era demands, while managing headcount with discipline.'
notes: ServiceNow's first layoff effort since CEO Bill McDermott's 2023 'no job cuts' pledge. Affected functions reportedly include solution consulting, sales, product marketing, and learning and development. Jobs estimate based on confirmed 'three-figure number' (low-hundreds), conservatively pegged at 300. Confirmed by Salesforce Ben, NowBen, and HRKatha reporting on June 11-12, 2026.

notes: Annual restructuring with AI investments cited

headline: Microsoft cuts 4,800 jobs in AI-driven restructuring
excerpt: Microsoft said on Monday it is cutting about 2.1% of its workforce, or roughly 4,800 jobs, as the Windows maker restructures parts of its commercial and Xbox businesses, joining other tech giants in a wave of layoffs as companies shift investment toward AI infrastructure.
notes: Part of annual July restructuring
